document_info.prepared_by: Patricia Nowak, Senior Broker, Steel City Insurance Group

business_description.nature_of_business: General contracting for commercial and institutional construction — healthcare facilities, educational buildings, and corporate offices throughout Western Pennsylvania

coverages_discussed.notes: Coverage requested for approximately 425 employees across all operations. 35 claims, $1,126,509.37 paid, $1,171,607.20 incurred over review period.
Occurrence form; includes standard premises/operations and products/completed operations. 8 claims, $105,420.70 paid, $176,946.61 incurred over review period.
15 vehicles including hired and non-owned auto. 8 claims, $47,076.38 paid, $99,900.04 incurred over review period.
All scheduled locations; special form with replacement cost valuation. 1 claim, $36,750.00 paid, $36,750.00 incurred over review period.

positives: Formal return-to-work program has reduced lost-time claims by over 30%
Dedicated safety committee meets monthly and conducts quarterly site audits
Formal business continuity and disaster recovery plan tested annually
ISO 9001 certification reflecting commitment to quality and process control
Contractual risk transfer provisions in all subcontractor and vendor agreements
Background checks and MVR reviews conducted annually for all employees in safety-sensitive positions
Structured HR program with 425 employees maintains low turnover and consistent safety training
42 years of continuous operations under consistent management demonstrating operational stability
concerns: Claims history warrants review per broker; management has implemented corrective measures
Account experienced a non-renewal/cancellation in 2022-2023 with Zurich Insurance Group due to carrier book of business exit
risk_highlights.loss_history_summary: Over the review period (5 loss years), the insured incurred 52 total claims with $1,315,756.45 in total paid losses and $1,485,203.85 total incurred. Currently 18 claims remain open and 34 have been closed. Annualized loss ratio is 18.7% (avg annual incurred $297,040.77 against current annual premium $1,589,164.70). Breakdown: Workers' Compensation — 35 claims, $1,126,509.37 paid, $1,171,607.20 incurred; General Liability — 8 claims, $105,420.70 paid, $176,946.61 incurred; Commercial Auto — 8 claims, $47,076.38 paid, $99,900.04 incurred; Commercial Property — 1 claim, $36,750.00 paid, $36,750.00 incurred.

extracted_text_sections.executive_summary: We are pleased to submit Keystone Commercial Builders, Inc. for your review and consideration. The insured is a general contracting for commercial and institutional construction — healthcare facilities, educational buildings, and corporate offices throughout Western Pennsylvania, headquartered in Pittsburgh, with approximately 42 years of operating history. Coverage is requested for Workers' Compensation, General Liability, Commercial Auto, Commercial Property.
extracted_text_sections.operations_description: General contracting for commercial and institutional construction — healthcare facilities, educational buildings, and corporate offices throughout Western Pennsylvania, headquartered in Pittsburgh. Approximately 425 employees across all operations.
extracted_text_sections.safety_programs: The insured has implemented a formal return-to-work program that has reduced lost-time claims by over 30%. The insured maintains a dedicated safety committee that meets monthly and conducts quarterly site audits. Management has invested significantly in employee safety training, with all field personnel OSHA-certified. The insured maintains a formal business continuity and disaster recovery plan tested annually. The organization has achieved ISO 9001 certification. The insured carries contractual risk transfer provisions in all subcontractor and vendor agreements. Background checks and MVR reviews are conducted annually for all employees in safety-sensitive positions.
extracted_text_sections.claims_narrative: The claims history warrants review; management has implemented corrective measures to address identified exposures. Over the review period, the insured has incurred 52 total claims with $1,315,756.45 in total paid losses and $1,485,203.85 total incurred. Currently, 18 claims remain open and 34 have been closed. Annualized loss ratio is 18.7% (avg annual incurred $297,040.77 against current annual premium $1,589,164.70 over 5 loss years). Note: account experienced a cancellation in 2022-2023 with Zurich Insurance Group. Reason: Carrier non-renewed — book of business exit.
